title: Check out this review
wikitext:
[]: Check out this review [NO SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:13 pm Damn this guy really didn't like 2.0, for those of you whop have seen it what do you think of this? http://www.colonydrop.com/index.php/2009/07/28/hideaki-anno-is-trying-to-kill-anime-and?blog=1 I think any spoilers that were int his are more or less common knowledge so it's probably ok to look at if you havent seent he movie.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:20 pm This should be locked and deleted for fear of homicidal rages.

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:24 pm Damn this is knit picky, lol. Frankly I'm sick of the whole "MARI RUINED DIS MOVIE" bitching. In fact, she was one of the factors that made this movie quite enjoyable, and honestly, its impossible to say this movie sucks because of a character whose back story hasn't been properly introduced yet (Which I'm assuming will happen in 3.0). I mean, I can see why he would think the original episodes were slightly better, but this review is ridiculous. He didn't even touch on the TRUE FLAWS of this movie, actually. All I'm hearing is more "THIS MOVIE IS A CASH COW AND THE NEW CHARACTER IS RUINING EVERYTHING RAAAAAAAAAAAAAAGE"

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:25 pm That's it, Im getting my Eva unit.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:53 pm Eh. He's entitled to his opinion. The internet allows everyone, including us, to express our own views on everything from movies to literature to political issues. If he hates Rebuild of Evangelion let him. Just cause he has a website doesn't mean his opinions are any more valid than anyone else's. His critiques are petty but they're HIS petty critiques. Personally, regarding Mari, I really liked the fresh (for EVA) element she added to the film but I do hope come part 3.0 she actually interacts with people other than Shinji and the Angles and Evangelions

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:58 pm Site is down. Someone be kind enough to weigh in on whether this review spoils anything. If it doesn't, I would like to read it.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:20 pm In case it has spoilers I am not reading it but from what Defectron says it sounds like the reviewer thinks "the original is good in every way. Only fucktard would want to see some crappy REMAKE!!!! ARGH!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!"

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:25 pm wonderluster wrote: This should be locked and deleted for fear of homicidal rages.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:40 pm Basically in a nutshell the review didn't delve into the plot at all it was just in bullet points- -NGE was stupid but tried to be intelligent so it was good. -Rebuild is a cash-in on a famous brand name (well DUH) so it's trash. -The animation is shitty (if I remember correctly he makes a mention of the camrip later on the review which leaves me suspect...) -Shikinami is not Sohryu -Hideaki Anno is a moron. -And so are you. Now there's a lot of cash in/brand name films I hate (Transformers 2) but I can't expect to be taken seriously in my criticism of the film if I say "You liked the film, then you're a moron". Which is what this guy gives the name of his review to. Like everything else, It's all a matter of taste.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:13 pm The more I hear about this the more it seems like the reviewer is an egocentric bastard who needs to personally approve material for fox news.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:59 pm WOW, what a lame article even if I agreed with anything in there regarding Rebuild (which I certainly do not), IMO he is either "just trying to be cool" by going against popular opinion or he's simply evolved into some kind of elitist/snobby connoisseur of art/entertainment and he's disgusted with even what he used to kind of like what bothers me the most (and everyone else like him including some on this forum) is that he so firmly believes he has Rebuild all figured out, in his mind it's clearly only about nursing Anno-sensei's ego and fattening some wallets everybody who's freaked out by Rebuild needs to just calm the fcuk down, realize it's not over yet, that there's 3.0/4.0 on the way and we all need to wait before this is all declared a dud Mari may very well be what Kaworu was in the original Eva, which was a plot device and not a character with depth (not to mention a merchandise ploy for the yaoi crowd), but it's too soon to write a character like Mari or all of Rebuild off completely

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Tue Aug 04, 2009 12:17 am I'm surprised that he thought Asuka and Rei became stereotypes. To me, their characters were a lot more interesting than their television counterparts.

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Tue Aug 04, 2009 1:20 am Geez what a prick time for Yui to bite him in half.

[]: Re: Check out this review [NO SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 04, 2009 1:27 am That review isn't a million miles from my reaction to the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Aug 04, 2009 3:52 am Like it was said before, everyone is entitled to have their own opinions, but i think the reviewers complaints about the animation, particularly the "Too much still shots!" are just ridiculous. There is a difference between using pans, tilts and closeups for effect/emphasis and using them because we have no clue on what to do right now. If anything, this movie's animation is hyperkinetic, rather than "sparse". 


[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:15 am That review made me wonder why he liked the original show in the first place. The only argument I found behind all that babbling was that the Rebuilds arent the same as the original show, and that's why they suck. And yet, at the same time, the Rebuilds are too similar to the original show, but executed more commercially. What? A reviewer cant make a mistake worse than insulting his readers. That's just low.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:22 am The guy seemed to pick up on a few genuine issues. I have to say I nodded my head more than once, but it does come off somewhat as a rant, and of course it doesn't help he seems to berate anime fans (surely his target audience). Although I have to admit I did enjoy film (possibly on a superficial level, but hey, better than waiting the thick end of 15 years to see a film you hate). Also this is the internet, everyone hates everything. A lot.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Aug 04, 2009 10:03 am I completely agree with him... but it doesnt stop me loving to see new eva. i think people need to stop trying to find hidden depth to it and just enjoy it for what it is... commercial eye candy that lets people see new material from their most loved characters without taking anything away from the original. None of the changes bother me. Why? Because its not NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 10:19 am Meh.... For shit and giggles I'm going to post my own here. "omigawd spoilerz ^___^" First I'll say though Mr. Tines, Jimmy etc. really, REALLY freaked me out before I saw film. I was expecting something utterly craptacular. When I didn't find the movie they had described I was doubly surprised. I was propably IMDB's boards biggest skeptic and in all honesty found pretty much only things to whine about in trailers etc. so here's the post: I never thought you would hear this from me: 2.0 is ****ing awesome So, I just saw the camrip. Twice. Pretty much completely spoilerless review follows. Oh Anno, how could I doubt you had lost your touch? Is Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance better than NGE eps its based on? Hell no. Is it grand, majestetic movie of epic proportions and pure win? Hell yeah. ***************************** My feelings regarding RoE 2.0 have been very ambivalent throughout. While 1.0 was awesome film it was for 90% same as original NGE - only with more modern animation and CGI which made me not to anticipate much more from 2.0. Even with the tremendous climax against Ramiel it wasn't as great as original episode 1-6. While 1.0 had managed without that much of dumbing down of the content and themes (it did though, but that's understandable. Besides RoE =/= NGE so not like it matters *that* much) I was very worried about 2.0. It was going to cover range of 12 episodes in 90 minutes, from trailers you could see it was full with angels (5 in total) and yet it was supposed somehow to pack in chockful of character development and interaction (otherwise this wouldn't be EVA), introduce Asuka, Kaji and the new character - wacky-gun-totin'-ho-with-glasses-and-grin Mari Makinami. Well, she ain't ho - not exactly - and she doesn't have gun - not exactly - but there's no other way to characterise this girl. Trailers themselves crushed my slowly growing anticipation. I thought (and still do since mj just retorted to ad hominems and repetition of same points, sorry man :) ) the visuals were bland, CGI overwrought and whole thing headed for fail. The heavy leaning on combat which this movie was going to have lots of anyway certainly didn't help. I was becoming sure this would be utter, incoherent mess with ridiculously fast pacing and dumbed down characters. Wasn't I surprised. **************************************************** This is great? Why yes, it is Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 is visually audacious and stunning film that completely reimagines and restructures the so called action arc and turning point (18-19) of the series. This is new stuff. While there are a lot of similar scenes or at least their blueprints nearly all of them are given at minimum new spin and often they are significantly different. And if ratio of seen-this-before in 1.0 was 95% to the 5% of new stuff you could nearly reverse the numbers for 2.0 With this work Rebuild becomes completely autonomous and free from shackles of original. I have despite the preview at the end of film no idea at all how the story will continue. In fact the similar surprise momentum was strong through the whole film as sequences of even familiar stuff were altered. For example we get scene from ep 15 pretty much right at the start yet stuff from ep 12 happens much later on. Anno's directing is in superb form. While he isn't as original, groundbreaking, experimental and flatout magnificent as NGE (after all you can invent certain techniques only once and you reach your absolute peak only once) which shone with majestetic and nigh unequivalent brilliance from editing to camerawork and cinematography to pacing and myriad of other aspects he makes only one glaring misstep in 2.0 which is less than in original show. Anno has always been genius who could make bad choices and but for two movies in a row "bad choices" are extremely limited. Camerawork and sound designs are brilliant and framings are simply put perfect from start to finish communicating themes of movie very succesfully. You won't find a single bad frame from this film or one that is out of place. His editing is also in major swing and while most of brilliance is recycle of NGEs themes and techniques the careful, attentive architecture with which he builds the more emotionally fragile and more brooding original material is simply put perfect. Anno has not lost his taste for deviously used alternative angels and artistic rhyming anywhere. And movie does break completely new ground when it comes to editing and animation once. I'm very confident to say Eva 2.0 features first time ever blatant, straight-from-Breathless styled jump cuts in history of animated films in one pivotal scene. Animation That's enough for formal side of directing. The animation muscle Khara shows in this movie is mindboggling. I wouldn't be surprised if for the grand fight scene that opens the movie they used as much money as for first half of the original show. And it never gets worse than that. Fluid flow and details never stop - when it comes to animating characters as a "actor" director Anno is only second to Miyazaki. This film is maximum eye candy from start to finish. Solving The Specific Problem of RoE 2.0 What however gathered most of my worries and most of my raised eyebrows for skill of handling of the matter was pacing and finding solution to problem of lightness/darkness. The action arc is most lightweight and happiest period of NGE but even it has its dark undertones and with eps 14 & 16 things get serious quick and fast. In episodic form this is easier, but how can you have the jolly good times and some of characteristic retardy humour in movie with apocalyptic gurggle and floods of blood (literally)? Most obvious answer wouldn've been to tone humour out nigh-completely or completely (Eva 1.0 is clearly less bouncy than eps 1-6 which weren't exactly Happy Days either) but that would've destroyed one of the most essential things in eva - the slow disintegration of our main characters facades and psyches. The contrast between Acion Arc and all that came later is one of show's greatest strenghts. The sickening violence of eps 18-19 would not be half as effective if whole show had been gloomy up to this point. I think that as a biggest proof for Anno's skill as director with this movie is that just how well he handles this problem. Film has fast pacing, but it never ever feels rushed. In fact it even feels rather calm in long sections in first half: and yet when one stops to think just how much has been happening here it isn't "calm" in traditional sense at all. Because I've been listening to music lately much more than watching films I'll offer analogy. I'd call the way Anno structured movie's first half or so (the "action arc" equivalent namely) minor/Major gambit. This is compositional technique Beatles pretty much "made up" (in context of popular music) in their early years - especially on A Hard Days' Night. They used the myriad ways it could spice up and bring original twists to harmonic material via continuously alternating between major and minor chords, namely writing progressions that went c-A-f-B instead of - say - C-A-F-B. This works as perfect analogy for how Anno mixes up more downbeat, "serious" material and the happy, sometimes even goofy moments together. The cheery classroom scene would be followed by Rei sitting all alone in her dark apartment at night, bathing in moonlight and holding mysterious pills in her hands. Then would again come "lighter" stuff which would be followed by more downbeat content. Anno's shifts and pacing between these two modes are marvelous. The feel of whole movie just changes completely, without leaving too much unfortunate cognitive dissonance. Film is absolutely blast to watch and I found out I had been watching film for nearly an hour when I thought it had been 20 minutes or so. It is absorbing work. The Gripes Khara screws up only once this procedure of minor/Major, in worst and completely unevangelion scene so far seen in official material. There are also some minor gripes - I have no idea what they were thinking when they added some frickin' looney tuney sound effects on two occasions. And few times (really few, countable on one hands fingers) they pull out "too anime" silly faces for short moments. But these are really minor gripes and do not harm the whole in any meaningful way. There is one loud, ugly chord in progression though, horrific remainder of worst aspects of animeism. For sake of common otaku stupidity (I come up short for other explanations) movie involves one horrible "humorous" scene. Parallel scene of Shinji's (in?)famous "toothpicks" incident. This time it features Asuka. After Asuka realizes Shinji has seen her naked she goes on crazy mode, jump kicks him in the face (in slow mo no less). Shinji collapses on floor with drool dripping to floor like complete retard. Misato just watches. Seriously, what the hell? Unfortunately I also have to include soundtrack under this section. It's a bit hit and miss and rather sad failure on Shiro Sagisu's part. I rank End of Evangelion's OST among best film soundtracks and while he has never been Joe Hisaishi this time around some of the results were really mixed bag. When it worked, it worked fantastically (we get couple of those "Komm Süsser Tod" moments towards the end to really, really creepy effect) but some tracks just feel they are out of place. Or they just aren't that great. There's this short, truly beautiful and atmospheric scene when morning sun shines upon Tokyo 3, people buzz out to the streets and houses rise from ground in stunning shot with people framed in foreground. Unfortunately otherwise brilliant track has some sort of disco drum going on in background making perfect scene to near-perfect. Movie also heavily recycles that of KK and for me that was problem of cognitive dissonance on first seeing. The funny tracks of KK suit the more humorous and warm contents brilliantly, but couple of piano movements just summoned in my head really strong pictures of our heroine in that show - not exactly best association when you're watching 2nd Impact to unfold. Luckily on 2nd viewthrough this wasn't anymore problem and e.g that 2nd Impact "flashback" (if you can call it such. [Sidenote: It is also only part of movie that features more experimental animation style.] It's also one of best scenes of movie, absolutely fantastic) worked well when I had got rid off my own associations. Happy sappy? One more point. Even with Anno's "minor/Major" scheme movie wouldn't have worked if the dark, twisted underbelly of show was *that* twisted and disgusting as it was. To reflect (like I suspected) the happier Anno this film is also more positive and "happier" than NGE. Nothing fundamental has changed (which becomes painfully clear when you reach hellish second half) but tones are lighter. To illustrate with example. Toji's sister in original show was never seen and apparently never ever got better, even after months and heck, nearly a year. She must've been at least paralysed in my mind. Girl's fate hovered in rather oppressive and sad manner in beginning of show and in eps 17-18. This was world in which puppies get kicked. In 2.0 however there is short, warm and absolutely lovely moment when the little girl gets out of hospital (with bunch of flowers) and Toji just shines with joy. It is heartwarming moment. It is this kind of small things that make difference. Other would be Shinji/Kaji/Melons scene. In original it was late evening and mood was melancholy and a bit opressive. The brilliant interplay between editing and silence made it all but very light scene. Contrast this with 2.0's one. It happens on beautiful, beautiful field with lake or sea glimmering in horizon in middle of couldless day. There's ethereal, floaty piano music in background instead of silence. It still maintains emotional problems and thematics of that brilliant scene, but the tone is warmer. Mindrape is also pretty much gone with one brilliant, glorious exception of hell train in second half. Unfortunately this "ligthness" also contributes to the fact film is not as transcendental as original NGE eps of the same "timeline". Ep 16 alone is still better than whole 2.0 and there's not single scene that's as mindbogglingly brilliant as e.g Rei's Poem and the ones that are are also found from original show. But the contents presented here are handled with absolute security and brilliance. And really, this is whining about movie being A- or A instead of A+ that is the original work. What every fan wants One more thing to top this all off: This is by far the most entertaining thing related to Evangelion ever. In fact I've barely ever encountered anything that works so well as pure entertainment. We get all fans want: Amazing, even a "bit" over-the-top (but never narmy or silly) action, new characters, fantastic character interactions etc. Film is pure joy to watch. 90 minutes went flying by and nowadays that happen barely ever with movies, no matter how good they are otherwise. Final Verdict Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance is stunning film of directorial prowess, animation feats and heavily engaging storyline and characters. It features twists upon twists to keep the old fan on the edge and reimagines NGE. I can not stress enough the crucial differences in which this movie ended in comparison with NGE ep 19. All is possible after this, all. And really, this is all based on camrip! I can barely wait to see this on DVD/BluRay 10/10 here's thread: I discuss some of other points and criticisms raised against the movie there and give overall thoughts with Jimbo and Oz and whatnot. http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0112159/board/flat/143389155?d=143389155#143389155 read if you want

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 10:19 am Meh.... For shit and giggles I'm going to post my own here. "omigawd spoilerz ^___^" First I'll say though Mr. Tines, Jimmy etc. really, REALLY freaked me out before I saw film. I was expecting something utterly craptacular. When I didn't find the movie they had described I was doubly surprised. I was propably IMDB's boards biggest skeptic and in all honesty found pretty much only things to whine about in trailers etc. so here's the post: I never thought you would hear this from me: 2.0 is ****ing awesome So, I just saw the camrip. Twice. Pretty much completely spoilerless review follows. Oh Anno, how could I doubt you had lost your touch? Is Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance better than NGE eps its based on? Hell no. Is it grand, majestetic movie of epic proportions and pure win? Hell yeah. ***************************** My feelings regarding RoE 2.0 have been very ambivalent throughout. While 1.0 was awesome film it was for 90% same as original NGE - only with more modern animation and CGI which made me not to anticipate much more from 2.0. Even with the tremendous climax against Ramiel it wasn't as great as original episode 1-6. While 1.0 had managed without that much of dumbing down of the content and themes (it did though, but that's understandable. Besides RoE =/= NGE so not like it matters *that* much) I was very worried about 2.0. It was going to cover range of 12 episodes in 90 minutes, from trailers you could see it was full with angels (5 in total) and yet it was supposed somehow to pack in chockful of character development and interaction (otherwise this wouldn't be EVA), introduce Asuka, Kaji and the new character - wacky-gun-totin'-ho-with-glasses-and-grin Mari Makinami. Well, she ain't ho - not exactly - and she doesn't have gun - not exactly - but there's no other way to characterise this girl. Trailers themselves crushed my

Article: Check out this review
'-ho-with-glasses-and-grin Mari Makinami. Well, she ain't ho - not exactly - and she doesn't have gun - not exactly - but there's no other way to characterise this girl. Trailers themselves crushed my slowly growing anticipation. I thought (and still do since mj just retorted to ad hominems and repetition of same points, sorry man :) ) the visuals were bland, CGI overwrought and whole thing headed for fail. The heavy leaning on combat which this movie was going to have lots of anyway certainly didn't help. I was becoming sure this would be utter, incoherent mess with ridiculously fast pacing and dumbed down characters. Wasn't I surprised. **************************************************** This is great? Why yes, it is Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 is visually audacious and stunning film that completely reimagines and restructures the so called action arc and turning point (18-19) of the series. This is new stuff. While there are a lot of similar scenes or at least their blueprints nearly all of them are given at minimum new spin and often they are significantly different. And if ratio of seen-this-before in 1.0 was 95% to the 5% of new stuff you could nearly reverse the numbers for 2.0 With this work Rebuild becomes completely autonomous and free from shackles of original. I have despite the preview at the end of film no idea at all how the story will continue. In fact the similar surprise momentum was strong through the whole film as sequences of even familiar stuff were altered. For example we get scene from ep 15 pretty much right at the start yet stuff from ep 12 happens much later on. Anno's directing is in superb form. While he isn't as original, groundbreaking, experimental and flatout magnificent as NGE (after all you can invent certain techniques only once and you reach your absolute peak only once) which shone with majestetic and nigh unequivalent brilliance from editing to camerawork and cinematography to pacing and myriad of other aspects he makes only one glaring misstep in 2.0 which is less than in original show. Anno has always been genius who could make bad choices and but for two movies in a row "bad choices" are extremely limited. Camerawork and sound designs are brilliant and framings are simply put perfect from start to finish communicating themes of movie very succesfully. You won't find a single bad frame from this film or

Article: Check out this review
 in a row "bad choices" are extremely limited. Camerawork and sound designs are brilliant and framings are simply put perfect from start to finish communicating themes of movie very succesfully. You won't find a single bad frame from this film or one that is out of place. His editing is also in major swing and while most of brilliance is recycle of NGEs themes and techniques the careful, attentive architecture with which he builds the more emotionally fragile and more brooding original material is simply put perfect. Anno has not lost his taste for deviously used alternative angels and artistic rhyming anywhere. And movie does break completely new ground when it comes to editing and animation once. I'm very confident to say Eva 2.0 features first time ever blatant, straight-from-Breathless styled jump cuts in history of animated films in one pivotal scene. Animation That's enough for formal side of directing. The animation muscle Khara shows in this movie is mindboggling. I wouldn't be surprised if for the grand fight scene that opens the movie they used as much money as for first half of the original show. And it never gets worse than that. Fluid flow and details never stop - when it comes to animating characters as a "actor" director Anno is only second to Miyazaki. This film is maximum eye candy from start to finish. Solving The Specific Problem of RoE 2.0 What however gathered most of my worries and most of my raised eyebrows for skill of handling of the matter was pacing and finding solution to problem of lightness/darkness. The action arc is most lightweight and happiest period of NGE but even it has its dark undertones and with eps 14 & 16 things get serious quick and fast. In episodic form this is easier, but how can you have the jolly good times and some of characteristic retardy humour in movie with apocalyptic gurggle and floods of blood (literally)? Most obvious answer wouldn've been to tone humour out nigh-completely or completely (Eva 1.0 is clearly less bouncy than eps 1-6 which weren't exactly Happy Days either) but that would've destroyed one of the most essential things in eva - the slow disintegration of our main characters facades and psyches. The contrast between Acion Arc and all that came later is one of show's greatest strenghts. The sickening violence of eps 18-19 would not be half as effective if whole show had

Article: Check out this review
 main characters facades and psyches. The contrast between Acion Arc and all that came later is one of show's greatest strenghts. The sickening violence of eps 18-19 would not be half as effective if whole show had been gloomy up to this point. I think that as a biggest proof for Anno's skill as director with this movie is that just how well he handles this problem. Film has fast pacing, but it never ever feels rushed. In fact it even feels rather calm in long sections in first half: and yet when one stops to think just how much has been happening here it isn't "calm" in traditional sense at all. Because I've been listening to music lately much more than watching films I'll offer analogy. I'd call the way Anno structured movie's first half or so (the "action arc" equivalent namely) minor/Major gambit. This is compositional technique Beatles pretty much "made up" (in context of popular music) in their early years - especially on A Hard Days' Night. They used the myriad ways it could spice up and bring original twists to harmonic material via continuously alternating between major and minor chords, namely writing progressions that went c-A-f-B instead of - say - C-A-F-B. This works as perfect analogy for how Anno mixes up more downbeat, "serious" material and the happy, sometimes even goofy moments together. The cheery classroom scene would be followed by Rei sitting all alone in her dark apartment at night, bathing in moonlight and holding mysterious pills in her hands. Then would again come "lighter" stuff which would be followed by more downbeat content. Anno's shifts and pacing between these two modes are marvelous. The feel of whole movie just changes completely, without leaving too much unfortunate cognitive dissonance. Film is absolutely blast to watch and I found out I had been watching film for nearly an hour when I thought it had been 20 minutes or so. It is absorbing work. The Gripes Khara screws up only once this procedure of minor/Major, in worst and completely unevangelion scene so far seen in official material. There are also some minor gripes - I have no idea what they were thinking when they added some frickin' looney tuney sound effects on two occasions. And few times (really few, countable on one hands fingers) they pull out "too anime" silly faces for short moments. But these are really minor gripes and do

Article: Check out this review
 frickin' looney tuney sound effects on two occasions. And few times (really few, countable on one hands fingers) they pull out "too anime" silly faces for short moments. But these are really minor gripes and do not harm the whole in any meaningful way. There is one loud, ugly chord in progression though, horrific remainder of worst aspects of animeism. For sake of common otaku stupidity (I come up short for other explanations) movie involves one horrible "humorous" scene. Parallel scene of Shinji's (in?)famous "toothpicks" incident. This time it features Asuka. After Asuka realizes Shinji has seen her naked she goes on crazy mode, jump kicks him in the face (in slow mo no less). Shinji collapses on floor with drool dripping to floor like complete retard. Misato just watches. Seriously, what the hell? Unfortunately I also have to include soundtrack under this section. It's a bit hit and miss and rather sad failure on Shiro Sagisu's part. I rank End of Evangelion's OST among best film soundtracks and while he has never been Joe Hisaishi this time around some of the results were really mixed bag. When it worked, it worked fantastically (we get couple of those "Komm Süsser Tod" moments towards the end to really, really creepy effect) but some tracks just feel they are out of place. Or they just aren't that great. There's this short, truly beautiful and atmospheric scene when morning sun shines upon Tokyo 3, people buzz out to the streets and houses rise from ground in stunning shot with people framed in foreground. Unfortunately otherwise brilliant track has some sort of disco drum going on in background making perfect scene to near-perfect. Movie also heavily recycles that of KK and for me that was problem of cognitive dissonance on first seeing. The funny tracks of KK suit the more humorous and warm contents brilliantly, but couple of piano movements just summoned in my head really strong pictures of our heroine in that show - not exactly best association when you're watching 2nd Impact to unfold. Luckily on 2nd viewthrough this wasn't anymore problem and e.g that 2nd Impact "flashback" (if you can call it such. [Sidenote: It is also only part of movie that features more experimental animation style.] It's also one of best scenes of movie, absolutely fantastic) worked well when I had got rid off my own associations.

Article: Check out this review
 you can call it such. [Sidenote: It is also only part of movie that features more experimental animation style.] It's also one of best scenes of movie, absolutely fantastic) worked well when I had got rid off my own associations. Happy sappy? One more point. Even with Anno's "minor/Major" scheme movie wouldn't have worked if the dark, twisted underbelly of show was *that* twisted and disgusting as it was. To reflect (like I suspected) the happier Anno this film is also more positive and "happier" than NGE. Nothing fundamental has changed (which becomes painfully clear when you reach hellish second half) but tones are lighter. To illustrate with example. Toji's sister in original show was never seen and apparently never ever got better, even after months and heck, nearly a year. She must've been at least paralysed in my mind. Girl's fate hovered in rather oppressive and sad manner in beginning of show and in eps 17-18. This was world in which puppies get kicked. In 2.0 however there is short, warm and absolutely lovely moment when the little girl gets out of hospital (with bunch of flowers) and Toji just shines with joy. It is heartwarming moment. It is this kind of small things that make difference. Other would be Shinji/Kaji/Melons scene. In original it was late evening and mood was melancholy and a bit opressive. The brilliant interplay between editing and silence made it all but very light scene. Contrast this with 2.0's one. It happens on beautiful, beautiful field with lake or sea glimmering in horizon in middle of couldless day. There's ethereal, floaty piano music in background instead of silence. It still maintains emotional problems and thematics of that brilliant scene, but the tone is warmer. Mindrape is also pretty much gone with one brilliant, glorious exception of hell train in second half. Unfortunately this "ligthness" also contributes to the fact film is not as transcendental as original NGE eps of the same "timeline". Ep 16 alone is still better than whole 2.0 and there's not single scene that's as mindbogglingly brilliant as e.g Rei's Poem and the ones that are are also found from original show. But the contents presented here are handled with absolute security and brilliance. And really, this is whining about movie being A- or A instead

Article: Check out this review
lingly brilliant as e.g Rei's Poem and the ones that are are also found from original show. But the contents presented here are handled with absolute security and brilliance. And really, this is whining about movie being A- or A instead of A+ that is the original work. What every fan wants One more thing to top this all off: This is by far the most entertaining thing related to Evangelion ever. In fact I've barely ever encountered anything that works so well as pure entertainment. We get all fans want: Amazing, even a "bit" over-the-top (but never narmy or silly) action, new characters, fantastic character interactions etc. Film is pure joy to watch. 90 minutes went flying by and nowadays that happen barely ever with movies, no matter how good they are otherwise. Final Verdict Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance is stunning film of directorial prowess, animation feats and heavily engaging storyline and characters. It features twists upon twists to keep the old fan on the edge and reimagines NGE. I can not stress enough the crucial differences in which this movie ended in comparison with NGE ep 19. All is possible after this, all. And really, this is all based on camrip! I can barely wait to see this on DVD/BluRay 10/10 here's thread: I discuss some of other points and criticisms raised against the movie there and give overall thoughts with Jimbo and Oz and whatnot. http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0112159/board/flat/143389155?d=143389155#143389155 read if you want
